purpose: Candidate public-domain quote sources for Bookshelf corpus expansion; must be title+author deduplicated against the existing corpus before ingestion.

provider.name: Standard Ebooks
provider.rights_class: The Standard Ebooks edition work is dedicated to the public domain via CC0 1.0; the underlying texts are believed to be in the U.S. public domain.
provider.jurisdiction_note: US-focused clearance only. Retain the exact source commit and independently assess distribution jurisdictions outside the United States.

extraction_contract.reliable_text_format: EPUB3 XHTML in each repository's src/epub/text/ directory
extraction_contract.include: Reading text and authorial epigraphs only
extraction_contract.exclude: Colophon, title page, imprint, copyright notices, endnotes unless explicitly authored text
extraction_contract.locator_strategy: Record the repository identifier, XHTML relative path and paragraph locator, normalized excerpt SHA-256, and extraction-snapshot SHA-256. Immutable commit pins were not captured for this release, so records remain review-pending.
extraction_contract.ingestion_gate: Reject if the repository's license/readme no longer states public-domain/CC0 status, if a title+author already exists, or if source text cannot be located exactly.

release_boundary: The imported records retain source identifiers, repository links, XHTML locators, excerpt digests, and extraction-snapshot digests, but the staging sources were not pinned to immutable repository commits. Every v2 record therefore remains source-linked-review-pending and must not be described as source-verified.
